note: These are LLM-generated detailed descriptions for pipeline activities. This data structure demonstrates the principle and can be integrated into the dashboard later.

get_table_list.brief: Retrieves the list of table names and metadata from the source Oracle database to determine which tables need to be extracted.
get_table_list.detailed: This Fabric Notebook connects to the Oracle database via established credentials, queries the data dictionary (ALL_TABLES or DBA_TABLES), and retrieves comprehensive metadata including table names, owner, row counts, and last modification timestamps. It performs initial validation to ensure connectivity and filters tables based on extraction criteria (inclusion/exclusion lists). The notebook generates a standardized dataframe with table metadata that feeds into downstream copy activities.
outputData: Dataframe containing list of tables
Metadata: table name, owner, row count, last modified date
Status flags for each table (ready_to_extract, skip_reason)
transformations: Query Oracle data dictionary for table information
Standardize output format for downstream consumption
qualityChecks: Verify database connectivity
Confirm retrieved table list is not empty
Validate metadata completeness
Check for duplicate entries
get_table_list.technicalNotes: Uses pyodbc or SQLAlchemy for database connectivity. May include performance optimization for large database schemas (> 1000 tables).

Copy_tables_over_gateway.brief: Iterates through each table identified and copies its data from the Oracle database via the data gateway into the lakehouse landing zone.
Copy_tables_over_gateway.detailed: This ForEach activity wraps a Copy Data activity that executes once for each table in the list generated by get_table_list. For each iteration, the Copy activity establishes a connection through the data gateway (avoiding direct external connections), reads the entire source table, and writes it to the lakehouse in Parquet format. The gateway provides secure, monitored data transfer with automatic compression and partitioning based on table size.
inputData: Source: Oracle tables via data gateway
outputData: Parquet files in lakehouse landing zone
File naming: [table_name]_[timestamp].parquet
Metadata: row count, column count, file size, extraction timestamp
transformations: Iterate over each table in collection
Extract full table data via secure gateway
Convert to Parquet format for optimal lakehouse storage
Auto-partition by table for easier downstream processing
qualityChecks: Verify source row count matches target row count
Check for null columns or data type mismatches
Validate file creation in lakehouse
Log any copy failures for retry logic
Copy_tables_over_gateway.technicalNotes: Gateway provides enterprise-grade security and monitoring. Copy activity includes error handling to enable pick-up-errors retry flow.

transfer_all_tab_columns_from_oracle.brief: Establishes connection to Oracle database and transfers table column definitions to the lakehouse for schema validation and mapping.
transfer_all_tab_columns_from_oracle.detailed: This Copy activity retrieves detailed column metadata from the Oracle data dictionary (ALL_TAB_COLUMNS or DBA_TAB_COLUMNS) including column names, data types, length, nullability, and default values. The column definitions are copied into the lakehouse to create a reference schema that downstream activities use for data type mapping, validation, and transformation logic. This ensures consistent handling of data types across the multi-step ETL process.
inputData: Filter criteria: owner, table name from previous extraction
outputData: CSV/Parquet containing column metadata
Columns: table_name, column_name, data_type, data_length, nullable, data_default, comments
transformations: Map Oracle data types to standardized data types
Flatten hierarchical metadata into tabular format
qualityChecks: Verify all extracted tables have column definitions
Check for columns with missing or null data types
Validate data type mappings are supported
transfer_all_tab_columns_from_oracle.technicalNotes: Column metadata is static and reusable across multiple runs, enabling cache-friendly optimization. Includes data type mapping utilities for downstream use.

Transfer all_tab_columns from lakehouse.brief: Moves the transferred column metadata from the lakehouse to the SQL warehouse for further processing and validation.
Transfer all_tab_columns from lakehouse.detailed: This Copy activity transfers the column schema metadata that was previously extracted from Oracle into the SQL warehouse staging tables. The SQL warehouse then uses this metadata to drive data validation, schema creation, and transformation logic. This creates a synchronized view of source system schema within the data warehouse, enabling metadata-driven processing and schema evolution tracking.
outputData: SQL table: stg_oracle_column_metadata
Columns: table_name, column_name, data_type, data_length, nullable, mapping_to_warehouse_type
transformations: Copy metadata from lakehouse to SQL
Create or update staging table schema
qualityChecks: Verify row count matches source
Check all required metadata columns present
Validate data type mapping completeness
Transfer all_tab_columns from lakehouse.technicalNotes: Staging table provides foundation for downstream data quality and schema management processes.

Truncate all_tab_columns.brief: Clears existing table column data from the SQL warehouse before loading fresh extracted metadata to ensure data consistency.
Truncate all_tab_columns.detailed: This Script activity executes a T-SQL TRUNCATE statement against the column metadata staging table in the SQL warehouse. Truncating (rather than deleting) is more efficient for large tables as it deallocates data pages. This step ensures a clean slate for the next metadata load cycle, preventing duplicate or stale metadata from interfering with downstream validation and schema creation processes. The truncate occurs after the fresh metadata has been validated but before the old records are purged.
outputData: Cleared staging table (0 rows)
Log entry: truncate timestamp, row count cleared
qualityChecks: Verify table exists and is accessible
Confirm truncate successful (0 rows remaining)
Check foreign key constraints (should not exist on staging tables)
Truncate all_tab_columns.technicalNotes: Truncate is preferred over DELETE for performance. Only executed after validation of fresh data to prevent data loss.

Log whole run complete.brief: Writes completion status and metrics to the logging system after successful data extraction and load operations.
Log whole run complete.detailed: This Script activity executes T-SQL commands that insert a completion record into the pipeline execution log table. The record captures run metadata: pipeline name, execution timestamp, status (success/failure), total tables extracted, total rows loaded, duration, and performance metrics. This logging enables operational monitoring, SLA tracking, and historical analysis of extraction patterns and performance trends.
inputData: Pipeline execution context: run_id, pipeline_name, start_time
outputData: Log entry in: tbl_pipeline_execution_log
Columns: run_id, pipeline_name, status, table_count, row_count, start_time, end_time, duration_mins
qualityChecks: Verify all required log fields populated
Check timestamp sequence (end > start)
Confirm log table is accessible and writable
Log whole run complete.technicalNotes: Log records enable dashboard monitoring, alerting, and performance analysis. Used to track SLA compliance and capacity planning.

Copy_tables_over_gateway_pick_up_errors.brief: Re-attempts to copy tables that failed in the initial extraction pass, implementing error recovery and resilience.
Copy_tables_over_gateway_pick_up_errors.detailed: This ForEach activity provides fault tolerance by re-attempting extraction of tables that failed during the initial Copy_tables_over_gateway pass. It iterates over the failed table list (identified by get_table_list_pick_up_errors), re-establishes the gateway connection with fresh credentials, and retries the copy operation. This implements a resilience pattern that handles transient failures (network timeouts, temporary unavailability) while distinguishing them from permanent failures.
inputData: Retry attempt number and delay settings
outputData: Retry Parquet files for previously failed tables
Updated status: succeeded_on_retry or failed_final
Error log entry if final retry also fails
transformations: Re-attempt copy with fresh gateway connection
qualityChecks: Track retry attempt count
Log error details for escalation
Verify no duplicate data if table was partially copied
Copy_tables_over_gateway_pick_up_errors.technicalNotes: Retry logic prevents minor transient issues from causing entire pipeline failure. Typically configured with 2-3 retry attempts with exponential backoff.

get_table_list_pick_up_errors.brief: Retrieves list of tables that encountered errors during initial extraction to enable targeted retry processing.
get_table_list_pick_up_errors.detailed: This Notebook queries the pipeline execution error log to identify which specific tables failed during the Copy_tables_over_gateway pass. It examines error codes, timestamps, and table identifiers to build a list of tables eligible for retry. The notebook applies business logic to distinguish transient failures (which merit retry) from permanent failures (which may require manual intervention). It outputs a filtered list passed to the retry ForEach activity.
inputData: Error codes and messages from failed copy activities
Retry eligibility criteria (error type, attempt count)
outputData: Dataframe: list of tables eligible for retry
Columns: table_name, error_code, error_message, retry_attempt, timestamp
transformations: Query error log for current run
Filter to relevant error codes (transient vs permanent)
qualityChecks: Verify error log table populated correctly
Check error codes are valid and mapped
Ensure retry list not empty (indicates all copies succeeded)
get_table_list_pick_up_errors.technicalNotes: Error categorization is critical—must distinguish transient (network, timeout) from permanent (permissions, schema) failures.

pl_finance_monthly_master.brief: Main orchestration step that coordinates the entire Finance monthly data pipeline by calling dependent silver and gold load procedures.
pl_finance_monthly_master.detailed: This ExecutePipeline activity serves as the master orchestrator for the complete Finance medallion architecture pipeline. It calls the pl_finance_silver_load pipeline, waits for completion, validates success, then triggers pl_finance_gold_load. This orchestration pattern ensures dependent pipelines execute in correct sequence, with data validation gates between layers. The master pipeline captures and logs execution status, enabling SLA tracking and operational visibility across the three medallion layers.
outputData: Completion status for entire Finance pipeline
Metrics: silver layer rows processed, gold layer rows created
Execution log: timestamps, statuses, error details
transformations: Route to error handling if sub-pipeline fails
qualityChecks: Verify silver layer completion before gold execution
Check row count ratios between layers (gold should be < silver)
Validate all transformation stages completed successfully
pl_finance_monthly_master.technicalNotes: Master orchestrator pattern enables modular pipeline design while maintaining coordinated execution and data integrity.

Start Email.brief: Sends notification email at pipeline start to notify stakeholders of execution beginning.
Start Email.detailed: This WebActivity sends an email notification at the start of pipeline execution to configured recipients (operations team, data stewards). The email includes pipeline name, scheduled run information, expected completion time, and action items if manual intervention is required. This enables proactive monitoring and alerting before long-running pipelines commence.
inputData: Pipeline metadata: name, schedule, estimated duration
outputData: Email sent to recipients
Delivery status logged
transformations: Format email body with pipeline details
Add conditional content (alerts if running outside normal schedule)
qualityChecks: Verify email sent successfully
Check recipient list not empty
Validate email formatting
Start Email.technicalNotes: Failure to send email typically does not block pipeline execution—logged as warning but continues.

email notification.brief: Sends completion email with run metrics, status, and any warning messages to configured recipients.
email notification.detailed: This WebActivity sends a summary email after pipeline completion containing execution status, performance metrics (duration, rows processed), and any warnings or errors encountered. The email provides business stakeholders with visibility into data pipeline health and enables rapid identification of issues requiring attention. Template includes links to logs, dashboards, and escalation procedures.
inputData: Pipeline execution results: status, start_time, end_time, row_counts
outputData: Completion email sent to recipients
Delivery confirmation logged
transformations: Calculate execution duration and performance metrics
Add relevant links and documentation references
qualityChecks: Verify all required metrics present
Check email template renders correctly
Validate recipient list
email notification.technicalNotes: Email sent regardless of success/failure to provide complete visibility. Critical failures may trigger escalation flags.

structure.brief: One-line description (what you see currently in dashboard)
structure.detailed: 2-3 sentence detailed explanation of what the activity actually does
structure.inputData: Array of inputs consumed by this activity
structure.outputData: Array of outputs produced by this activity
structure.qualityChecks: Array of data quality or validity checks performed

integrationNotes.howToUse: This JSON can be loaded into the dashboard code. Modify the 'selectActivity' function to check for activity details here before defaulting to brief descriptions.
integrationNotes.expandUIOption: Add a '>>> More details' link next to 'What This Does' that expands to show detailed, inputs, outputs, transformations, qualityChecks sections.
integrationNotes.nextSteps: 1. Review these descriptions for accuracy. 2. Add or modify descriptions as needed. 3. Integrate into dashboard code. 4. Add UI toggle for expanded view.
